evals.name: session-end-retrospective
evals.prompt: Let's wrap up. This session built skill eval infrastructure: eval-runner.py, grader.py, watch-evals.py, and session-import.py. All CI green, one file committed.
evals.expected_output: Full 6-section retrospective (≥800 words), specific MEMORY.md update proposed, Notion log offered. Efficiency section names specific missteps, not generic praise.

assertions.name: notion_log_offered
assertions.description: Output offers to log to Notion

assertions.name: memory_update_specific
assertions.description: Output proposes a specific MEMORY.md edit, not just mentions it

assertions.name: efficiency_specificity
assertions.description: Efficiency section names specific missteps, not generic comments
assertions.rubric: Pass if the efficiency section names at least one concrete misstep, wrong approach, or unnecessary round-trip (e.g. 'tried X before realizing Y', 'should have read Z first', 'cost an extra round-trip by...'). Fail if the section is generic ('the session went smoothly', 'prompts were clear', 'no major inefficiencies') without naming at least one specific moment.

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: No em dashes in the retrospective output

evals.name: session-start-brief
evals.prompt: Let's start a new session. What should we work on?
evals.expected_output: Session Brief with 4 sections: Active Notion Tasks, Git Status, CI Health, Recommended Next Step. Priority logic: CI failures surfaced first.

assertions.name: session_brief_present
assertions.description: Output contains a Session Brief heading

assertions.name: recommended_next_step
assertions.description: A recommended next step is given

assertions.name: notion_tasks_queried
assertions.description: Notion tasks are mentioned or queried as part of the brief
